official_title: DIFC Courts Order No. 1 of 2019 in Respect of Issuing and Conducting Proceedings, Rights of Audience and Registration in Part I and Part II of the DIFC Courts’ Register of Legal Practitioners

short_title: Courts Order No. 1 of 2019 in Respect of Issuing and Conducting Proceedings, Rights of Audience and Registration in Part I and Part II of the DIFC Courts’ Register of Legal Practitioners MAY 22, 2019 COURT ADMINISTRATIVE ORDERS I, Zaki Bin Azmi, of the Chief Justice (/glossary/chief-justice/) DIFC Courts (/glossary/DIFC-courts/) After having reviewed: Dubai Law No. 9 of 2004, as amended in respect of the establishment of the ; DIFC (/glossary/DIFC/) Dubai Law No. 12 of 2004, as amended in respect of the DIFC Courts; And having considered: DIFC Courts Order No. 1 of 2005 with respect to Rights to Conduct Proceedings and Rights of Audience; – DIFC Courts Order No. 1 of 2012 in respect of Rights to Conduct Proceedings and Rights of Audience before th DIFC Courts; ’s Direction No 2 of 2013 Issuing and Conducting Proceedings; Registrar (/glossary/registrar/) DIFC Courts Order No. 1 of 2015 in Respect of Rights of Audience and Registration in Part II of the Courts ’ Register of ; (/glossary/court/) Practitioners (/glossary/practitioner/) DIFC Courts Order No. 1 of 2016 in Respect of Rights of Audience and Registration in Part II of the Courts’ Register of Practitioners; DRA Order No. 1 of 2018 in Respect of Rights of Audience and Registration in Part II of the Academy of Law’s Register of Practitioners; Hereby, pursuant to the powers vested in me by Article 8 (5) (b) of Dubai Law No. 7 of 2014, amendingDubai Law No. 9 of 2004 and according to Resolution No. (1) of 2019 In Respect of the Dispute solution Authority Academy, issue the following Order: 1. This Order may be cited as DIFC Courts Order No. 1 of 2019 in Respect of Issuing and Conducting Proceedings, Rights of Audience and egistration in Part I and Part II of the DIFC Courts’ Register of Legal Practitioners which amends the Academy of Law’s Register of Practitioners 2. 1. All legal registered in the Academy of Law’s Register of Legalpractitioners (/glossary/practitioner/) Practitioners prior to the transfer of the register to the DIFC Courts shall continue to be registered

DIFC Courts Order No. 1 of 2019 in Respect of Issuing and Conducting Proceedings, Rights of Audience and Registration in Part I and Part II of the DIFC Courts’ Register of Legal Practitioners 2019

Courts Order No. 1 of 2019 in Respect of Issuing and Conducting Proceedings, Rights of Audience and Registration in Part I and Part II of the DIFC Courts’ Register of Legal Practitioners MAY 22, 2019 COURT ADMINISTRATIVE ORDERS I, Zaki Bin Azmi, of the Chief Justice (/glossary/chief-justice/) DIFC Courts (/glossary/DIFC-courts/) After having reviewed: Dubai Law No. 9 of 2004, as amended in respect of the establishment of the ; DIFC (/glossary/DIFC/) Dubai Law No. 12 of 2004, as amended in respect of the DIFC Courts; And having considered: DIFC Courts Order No. 1 of 2005 with respect to Rights to Conduct Proceedings and Rights of Audience; – DIFC Courts Order No. 1 of 2012 in respect of Rights to Conduct Proceedings and Rights of Audience before th DIFC Courts; ’s Direction No 2 of 2013 Issuing and Conducting Proceedings; Registrar (/glossary/registrar/) DIFC Courts Order No. 1 of 2015 in Respect of Rights of Audience and Registration in Part II of the Courts ’ Register of ; (/glossary/court/) Practitioners (/glossary/practitioner/) DIFC Courts Order No. 1 of 2016 in Respect of Rights of Audience and Registration in Part II of the Courts’ Register of Practitioners; DRA Order No. 1 of 2018 in Respect of Rights of Audience and Registration in Part II of the Academy of Law’s Register of Practitioners; Hereby, pursuant to the powers vested in me by Article 8 (5) (b) of Dubai Law No. 7 of 2014, amendingDubai Law No. 9 of 2004 and according to Resolution No. (1) of 2019 In Respect of the Dispute solution Authority Academy, issue the following Order: 1. This Order may be cited as DIFC Courts Order No. 1 of 2019 in Respect of Issuing and Conducting Proceedings, Rights of Audience and egistration in Part I and Part II of the DIFC Courts’ Register of Legal Practitioners which amends the Academy of Law’s Register of Practitioners 2. 1. All legal registered in the Academy of Law’s Register of Legalpractitioners (/glossary/practitioner/) Practitioners prior to the transfer of the register to the DIFC Courts shall continue to be registered

Courts Order No. 1 of 2019 in Respect of Issuing and Conducting Proceedings, Rights of Audience and Registration in Part I and Part II of the DIFC Courts’ Register of Legal Practitioners MAY 22, 2019 COURT ADMINISTRATIVE ORDERS I, Zaki Bin Azmi, of the Chief Justice (/glossary/chief-justice/) DIFC Courts (/glossary/DIFC-courts/) After having reviewed: Dubai Law No. 9 of 2004, as amended in respect of the establishment of the ; DIFC (/glossary/DIFC/) Dubai Law No. 12 of 2004, as amended in respect of the DIFC Courts; And having considered: DIFC Courts Order No. 1 of 2005 with respect to Rights to Conduct Proceedings and Rights of Audience; – DIFC Courts Order No. 1 of 2012 in respect of Rights to Conduct Proceedings and Rights of Audience before th DIFC Courts; ’s Direction No 2 of 2013 Issuing and Conducting Proceedings; Registrar (/glossary/registrar/) DIFC Courts Order No. 1 of 2015 in Respect of Rights of Audience and Registration in Part II of the Courts ’ Register of ; (/glossary/court/) Practitioners (/glossary/practitioner/) DIFC Courts Order No. 1 of 2016 in Respect of Rights of Audience and Registration in Part II of the Courts’ Register of Practitioners; DRA Order No. 1 of 2018 in Respect of Rights of Audience and Registration in Part II of the Academy of Law’s Register of Practitioners; Hereby, pursuant to the powers vested in me by Article 8 (5) (b) of Dubai Law No. 7 of 2014, amendingDubai Law No. 9 of 2004 and according to Resolution No. (1) of 2019 In Respect of the Dispute solution Authority Academy, issue the following Order: 1. This Order may be cited as DIFC Courts Order No. 1 of 2019 in Respect of Issuing and Conducting Proceedings, Rights of Audience and egistration in Part I and Part II of the DIFC Courts’ Register of Legal Practitioners which amends the Academy of Law’s Register of Practitioners 2. 1. All legal registered in the Academy of Law’s Register of Legalpractitioners (/glossary/practitioner/) Practitioners prior to the transfer of the register to the DIFC Courts shall continue to be registered 2019

Article 1.heading: This Order may be cited as DIFC Courts Order No. 1 of 2019 in Respect of Issuing and Conducting Proceedings, Rights of Audience and egistration in Part I and Part II of the DIFC Courts’ Register of Legal Practitioners which amends the Academy of Law’s Register of Practitioners

Article 2.heading: Upon application to the Registrar, an applicant seeking to be registered as a legal practitioner under this Order who successfully completes training programmes that the DIFC(/glossary/practitioner/) Courts has specifi cally designated from time to time and who meet all other criteria for admission, may receive two years credit toward achieving limited or full rights of audience before the DIFC Courts

Article 3.heading: The DIFC Courts maintain a Register of those persons authorised to issue and conduct proceedings and/or to appear before the DIFC Courts on behalf of others. The registration requirements are set out in the Guidelines for Registration of Practitioners, as amended from time to time at the discretion of the Chief Justice of the DIFC Courts

Article 4.heading: The fees in respect of entry in the Register will be prescribed by published in the Guidelines for the DIFC Courts Register of Legal Practitioners on the DIFC Courts website, as amended from time to time in the discretion of the DIFC Courts

Article 5.heading: The Register is Legal Practitioners is in two Parts:

Article 6.heading: Entry in Part I is in the name of a fi rm and the fi rm provides a list of practitioners employed by the fi rm who are authorized to issue and conduct proceedings on behalf of the fi rm

Article 7.heading: A Part II application for entry in the Register must be made to the DIFC Courts, supported by but not limited to the following requirements:

Article 8.heading: 1. A minimum of fi ve years’ advocacy experience grants full rights of audience and a minimum of two years’ experience grants limited rights of audience before the DIFC Courts

Article 9.heading: A refusal by the DIFC Courts to register an applicant may be reviewed by the Chief Justice

Article 10.heading: With respect to an individual applying for registration in Part II of the Register, the procedure will be as follows: a. Registration in Part II of the Register will be provisional1 until the individual concerned appears before a judge . (/glossary/judge/)

Article 11.heading: An individual applying for registration in Part II of the Register should give the not Registry (/glossary/registry/) less than 7 days’ notice of his/her intention to appear before a judge, so that the necessary arrangements can be made to ensure the presence of the Registrar or Senior Legal Offi cer. If it is not possible to give suffi cient notice, or if no Registrar or Senior Legal Offi cer is available to attend the fi rst hearing, the individual will need to seek permission of the hearing judge to proceed with the hearing in the absence of the Registrar or Senior Legal Offi cer and the individual’s registration in the Register shall remain provisional until the next hearing, at which the Registrar or Senior Legal Offi cer shall attend and either: (i) confi rm the applicant’s registration as permanent; or (ii) refuse the registration (iii) request that the applicant considers a training on DIFC Courts Laws and Procedures and/or Advocacy training provided by the DIFC Courts or other recognised training provider in which case the individual’s registration shall remain provisional until the next hearing

Article 12.heading: This Order shall come into force with effect from 5 March 2019

This Order may be cited as DIFC Courts O.heading: This Order may be cited as DIFC Courts Order No. 1 of 2019 in Respect of Issuing and Conducting Proceedings, Rights of Audience and egistration in Part I and Part II of the DIFC Courts’ Register of Legal Practitioners which amends the Academy of Law’s Register of Practitioners

1. All legal registered in the Academy o.heading: 1. All legal registered in the Academy of Law’s Register of Legalpractitioners (/glossary/practitioner/) Practitioners prior to the transfer of the register to the DIFC Courts shall continue to be registered

All legal practitioners registered in th.heading: All legal practitioners registered in the Academy of Law’s Register of Legal Practitioners prior to the transfer of the register to the DIFC Courts shall continue to be registered
